question: Will two Republicans win in California's June 2026 gubernatorial primary?
textDescription: California primaries are nonpartisan, and the top two vote getters move on to the November election. If two Republicans win the primary, Californians will have no choice but to elect a Republican governor.
newsShortReason: Recent polls show two Republicans leading in California's gubernatorial primary.
newsKeyEvidence: Steve Hilton and Chad Bianco are leading in recent polls for California's gubernatorial primary. ([latimes.com](https://www.latimes.com/california/story/2026-02-25/california-governors-race-wealth-tax-congress-ppic-poll?utm_source=openai)) | The top two vote-getters in California's nonpartisan primary system advance to the general election, regardless of party affiliation.
